When I was learning JavaScript, one of the most painful, head-scratching things was `this`. It changes depending on where and how you call it, and that drove me up the wall. So I sat down and worked through a bunch of examples to figure out how `this` actually behaves — global scope, inside objects, strict mode, and the arrow function gotcha that bit me in real code.
